objective: Desktop-only Java/Unidbg signer with configurable observable Android/native state, unchanged Signer API, fixed onResume-to-sign order, frozen device-reference parity, and an independently executable recovered implementation including API-selected crypto behavior where proven.
currentFocus: Map remaining protected final-envelope control flow and unnamed correction/probe branches; v3/v3.1 certificates, importable API18-22 RSA state, and field0 growth through 24 halfwords are closed.

evidence.desktopExecution: Default Unidbg/original-SO and recovered C++ backends run fully on the host without a phone, ADB, Frida, Android emulator, or automatic system plugin installation.
evidence.apiAndLifecycle: Original public Signer descriptors pass test-api-contract.sh and SignerEngine preserves fixed onResume then sign ordering.
evidence.strictReference: Default and recovered backends both exactly match the complete frozen Pixel 8 structured reference; frozen SHA-256 remains b163eb800b2a425158f6b825e8e9439b4b9bd1bca8b0ed0be2a155f2ef9ceca0.
evidence.cmdlineCorrections: Controlled original-SO oracles prove missing or empty /proc/self/cmdline emits 0x34; a non-empty first NUL-terminated process name unequal to runtime packageName emits 0x09; a matching process name emits neither. APK manifest mismatch alone does not emit 0x09.
evidence.legacyApi: API 18, 21 and 22 now complete original-SO signing on the desktop through SharedPreferences, Base64, AndroidKeyStore RSA PrivateKeyEntry, RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding, SecretKeySpec and HmacSHA256 JNI support. Equal downstream observations produce identical 176-byte adj8 output.
evidence.cryptoSwitch: API 23+ obtains key2 through AndroidKeyStore.getKey then HmacSHA256; API 18-22 decrypt SharedPreferences encrypted_key through RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding before HmacSHA256. A deterministic direct original-SO matrix proves that changing the Java HMAC argument across 0/16/31/32/33/64 bytes or changing its contents does not alter the reached API36 result. API23-to-24 adds correction 0x3c and expands the payload, but retains algorithm=adj8 and the recovered custom-SHA256/AES-256-CBC/HMAC-SHA256 final envelope.
evidence.algorithmMetadata: Static disassembly corrects 0x9279c to the onResume context-bit setter. The actual protected metadata item builder is 0x9954c; four calls pass fixed encoded buffers that decode to headers_id=9, adj_signing_id=1400000, native_version=3.67.0 and algorithm=adj8. API22, API36, combined corrections and a 27-case parameter matrix preserve the same addresses and values.
evidence.v3Certificates: Project-owned pure Java ApkSigningBlockCertificates parses v3 and v3.1 APK Signing Blocks without Android SDK runtime tools. A v3-only APK with matching certificate reproduces the original-SO no-0x2a output; a mismatching certificate reproduces the original-SO 0x2a output; both complete 176-byte outputs match recovered backend byte-for-byte.
evidence.legacyRsaImport: device.legacyKeyStore imports a paired PKCS8 RSA private key and X509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo public key; device.sharedPreferences supplies adjust_keys/encrypted_key. A controlled API18 original-SO test decrypts the imported persisted secret and reproduces the complete 176-byte oracle.
evidence.configSurface: device.sharedPreferences configures named string preference files; native filesystem config controls /proc/self/cmdline and maps; recovered backend derives 0x34/0x09 and 0x3c for every observed API not equal to 36.
evidence.nativeSourceParity: Independent C++ matches thirteen original-SO oracle categories, including cmdline 0x09, cmdline 0x34, frozen 176-byte Pixel, nine-correction 192-byte expansion and seventeen-correction 208-byte expansion. Field0 capacity grows 8 to 16 to 24 to 32 halfwords in blocks of eight.
evidence.regression: After rebuilding the recovered C++ executable, the latest Maven build passes 64 tests with zero failures/errors/skips; v3/v3.1 parser is 3/3, recovered integration is 16/16 and legacy API integration is 2/2. API contract, frozen device-reference, recovered reference and native C++ oracle suite pass. The frozen reference SHA-256 remains b163eb800b2a425158f6b825e8e9439b4b9bd1bca8b0ed0be2a155f2ef9ceca0.
evidence.remainingBoundary: No reached profile proves a second final envelope algorithm. Remaining work is protected/unreached final-envelope control flow, unnamed correction/probe semantics, and broader full-function/protected-VM source recovery.
